publishedAbstract: * The scope of this document applies to two different scenarios: 1. Mons are still up, but the usage of the file system `/var/lib/ceph/mon/ceph-X` inside a mon pod is getting bigger. 2. Mons are crashing because the above file system doesn't have the minimum required free space. oc get pods-n openshift-storage | grep mon rook-ceph-mon-a-b7dffbfbb-9rxxb 1/1 Running 45 rook-ceph-mon-b-68cd5f9786-zvxp7 0/1 Error 1630 rook-ceph-mon-c-9f5b9885d-4tm9w 0/1 CrashLoopBackOff 1638 Reviewing the mon logs, the reason is clear: the mon file system is below a 4% free, which is the minimum threshold: oc logs rook-ceph-mon-c-9f5b9885d-4tm9w [...] debug 2021-02-03 09:24:10.944 7fee4c5ce3c0 0 setuser_match_path /var/lib/ceph/mon/ceph-c/store.db owned by 167:167. set uid:gid to 167:167 (ceph:ceph) debug 2021-02-03 09:24:10.944 7fee4c5ce3c0 0 ceph version 14.2.8-91.el8cp (75b4845da7d469665bd48d1a49badcc3677bf5cd) nautilus (stable), process ceph-mon, pid 1 debug 2021-02-03 09:24:10.944 7fee4c5ce3c0-1 error: monitor data filesystem reached concerning levels of available storage space (available: 4% 454 MiB) [...] * How to free up some space for both these cases?
publishedTitle: How to free up space for the mon filesystem in OCS 4.x?
